topics: 🚫 Trade in forbidden items: Torah-prohibited vs. rabbinically-prohibited
🐕 R' Yose b. R' Chanina on the spiritual cost of raising a vicious dog
🕊️ Pigeon-trap distances: 30 ריס vs. 50 אמה — feeding vs. flying ranges
✋ The Mishnah's five categories: נזק, צער, ריפוי, שבת, בושת
👁️ Why עין תחת עין must mean money — and the limit case of the blind/amputee

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Five Categories of Bodily Damage
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: The daf finishes the perek-7 cleanup (forbidden trade, the dangerous dog, pigeon-trap distances) and then opens perek 8 — the framework of bodily damage. The famous Mishnah lists five categories: נזק, צער, ריפוי, שבת, בושת. The Yerushalmi shows how they stack (5/4/3/2/1 depending on the injury), and confronts the eye-for-an-eye verses head-on, proving that the Torah requires monetary payment, not literal mutilation.
overview.hebrewSource: הַחוֹבֵל בַּחֲבֵירוֹ חַיָיב עָלָיו מִשּׁוּם חֲמִשָּׁה דְבָרִים בְּנֶזֶק בְּצַעַר בְּרִיפּוּי בְּשֶׁבֶת וּבְבוֹשֶׁת

slides.title: 🚫 Trade in Forbidden Items
slides.subtitle: Two pesukim, two prohibitions — and a key distinction.

boxes.title: 📜 The doubling of pesukim
items: Yerushalmi: one for אכילה, one for הנאה / סחורה

boxes.title: ❌ Torah-forbidden
items: But: the donkey and the camel?
Answer: לִמְלַאכְתּוֹ הוּא גָדֵל — they are raised for work, not as merchandise

callout.label: How the rule is salvaged:
callout.text: The donkey and camel seem to disprove the rule, but the Yerushalmi answers that they are raised for labor, not as goods to be traded — so the principle still stands.

slides.title: 🕊️🐕 Settlement Animals — Dogs and Pigeons
slides.subtitle: Two final perek-7 cases sharpen what 'protecting the public' means.

lines.speaker: M
lines.text: לֹא יְגַדֵּל אָדָם אֶת הַכֶּלֶב אֶלָּא אִם כֵּן קָשׁוּר בְּשַׁלְשֶׁלֶת — no dog without a chain.

lines.speaker: RY
lines.text: R' Yose b. R' Chanina: about anyone who raises a vicious dog the verse says לַמָּס מֵרֵעֵהוּ חָסֶד — he withholds kindness from his neighbor.

lines.speaker: Q
lines.text: Pigeons: here we say 30 ריס from a settlement, but elsewhere only 50 אמה. Which is it?

lines.speaker: RY
lines.text: R' Yose b. R' Bun: לִרְעוֹת מְרֵיעָה נֹ אַמָּה. לִפְרוֹחַ פּוֹרַחַת אֲפִילוּ אַרְבָּעַת מִילִין — they feed within 50 amah but fly up to four mil. The two distances measure two different ranges.

callout.label: Reading principle:
callout.text: Apparent contradictions in shiurim usually point to two different physical realities. Feeding range and flying range need different protection radii.

slides.title: 📊 The Five Categories of Damage — and How They Stack
slides.subtitle: From the beraita: 5 / 4 / 3 / 2 / 1 — based on the type of blow.
rows: Hit on the hand and severed it
Hit on the hand and it swelled
צער, ריפוי, שבת, בושת (no permanent נזק)
With a tome (folder/papers) in his hand

callout.label: What the gradation shows:
callout.text: Each category has its own trigger. נזק requires a permanent loss of capacity (assessed as if he were sold as a slave). בושת can be triggered even without any physical contact at all — a humiliating gesture is enough.

slides.title: 👁️ Why עין תחת עין Must Mean Money
slides.subtitle: The Yerushalmi's tight derivation from the doubling of שוגג and מזיד.

nodes.content: Two verses: עַיִן תַּחַת עַיִן + לֹא תָחוֹס עֵינֶךָ
One refers to שוגג, one refers to מזיד — both must be stated

columns.label: Only שוגג stated?
columns.content: Then I'd say מזיד actually has his hand cut off

columns.label: Only מזיד stated?
columns.content: Then I'd say שוגג pays nothing

nodes.content: Limit case: a blind man who blinded another, an amputee who severed an arm — הֵיאַךְ זֶה מִתְקַיֵים וַעֲשִׂיתֶם לוֹ כַּאֲשֶׁר זָמַם?
מַגִּיד שֶׁאֵינוֹ מְשַׁלֵּם אֶלָּא מָמוֹן — therefore the Torah's measure must be money.

callout.label: The clincher:
callout.text: If the Torah's penalty were literal mutilation, it would fail in cases where the perpetrator has nothing to lose (a blind man, an amputee). The Torah's principle of measure-for-measure only works if the payment is monetary.

slides.title: 💸 How to Assess צער and ריפוי
slides.subtitle: Three voices on appraising pain — and an aside on a Roman.

steps.content: 1️⃣ R' Ze'ira: ask the victim — how much would you pay to avoid this pain? That's the figure.
2️⃣ Mar Ukva: the Mishnah's wording is כַּמָּה אָדָם רוֹצֶה לִיטּוֹל — how much would he take to accept this pain? The roles are flipped, but the number is similar.
3️⃣ Ben Azai: include נִכְאֵי מָזוֹן — convalescent food. Before, lentils sufficed; now only eggs and chicken. The mazik pays the upgrade.
4️⃣ R' Yose b. Yaakov's vignette: a Roman soldier wouldn't go to battle until he had eaten back the days he'd missed. Pain restitution mirrors the body's own recovery economy.
5️⃣ ריפוי from the next Mishnah: includes complications (צמחים) and re-injuries (חָיְיתָה וְנִסְתְּרָה) caused by the original blow.

callout.label: Bottom line:
callout.text: The Yerushalmi treats damages as a recovery package, not a flat fee. Pain assessment, food upgrades, and ongoing medical care all flow from the original act of injury.

summary.principle: Perek 8 opens with the five categories of bodily damage. The Yerushalmi shows how they stack by type of blow, and proves rigorously that עין תחת עין must mean monetary compensation.

flowSteps.content:
Mishnah opens perek 8:
חֲמִשָּׁה דְבָרִים: נֶזֶק, צַעַר, רִיפּוּי, שֶׁבֶת, בּוֹשֶׁת

columns.content:
✋ 5/4/3/2/1
Categories owed depend on the type of blow

👁️ עין תחת עין
Must mean מָמוֹן (the blind/amputee proof)

flowSteps.content: Earlier on the daf: forbidden trade, vicious dogs, pigeon-trap distances — communal-protection halakhot.

🎓 Key Principle:
Bodily damage compensation is a layered, monetary package — calibrated to the injury's enduring effects on body, livelihood, and dignity.

quiz.q: Why does the Yerushalmi say the Torah doubled טְמֵאִים הֵם לָכֶם with וּטְמֵאֵים יִהְיוּ לָכֶם?
o: One forbids eating, one forbids הנאה (and so trade)
One refers to live animals, one to carcasses
quiz.e: אֶחָד אִיסּוּר אֲכִילָה וְאֶחָד אִיסּוּר הֲנָיָיה. Anything Torah-forbidden is also forbidden for trade; rabbinic prohibitions allow trade. The donkey/camel objection is answered by 'they are raised for work, not as merchandise.'

quiz.q: According to R' Yose b. R' Bun, why does one Mishnah say 30 ריס for pigeon traps and another 50 אמה?
o: 50 אמה is the feeding range, 30 ריס (or 4 mil) is the flight range
30 ריס applies to Eretz Yisrael only
The 50 אמה refers to the dovecote itself
quiz.e: לִרְעוֹת מְרֵיעָה נֹ אַמָּה. לִפְרוֹחַ פּוֹרַחַת אֲפִילוּ אַרְבָּעַת מִילִין — feeding range vs. flight range explain the two figures.

quiz.q: If someone hits another on the head and it swells visibly, how many categories of payment apply?
quiz.e: Per the beraita: head + swelling visible = 3 (no נזק because there's no permanent loss of capacity assessed כעבד נמכר; no בושת unless there's an embarrassment context). Five only when a limb is severed.

quiz.q: What is the Yerushalmi's strongest proof that עַיִן תַּחַת עַיִן means money?
o: The case of a blind man who blinds another, or an amputee who severs an arm — literal mutilation can't be applied uniformly
Because the Torah always means money
Because the Sanhedrin abolished bodily punishments
quiz.e: אִם הָיָה סוּמֶא וְסִימָּא אֵת עֵינוֹ... הֵיאַךְ זֶה מִתְקַיֵים וַעֲשִׂיתֶם לוֹ כַּאֲשֶׁר זָמַם. The principle of measure-for-measure breaks down unless the payment is monetary — מַגִּיד שֶׁאֵינוֹ מְשַׁלֵּם אֶלָּא מָמוֹן.

quiz.q: Ben Azai's נִכְאֵי מָזוֹן refers to what?
o: Convalescent food upgrades — e.g., previously lentils sufficed, now only eggs and chicken
quiz.e: Ben Azai adds that the mazik pays for the dietary upgrades the victim now needs — בְּקַדְמִיתָא הֲוִינָא אֲכִיל טְלוֹפְחִין וְיָרָק. וּכְדוֹן לֵית בִּי מֵיכַל אֶלָּא בֵּיעִין וְתַרְנוֹגַלִּין.
